ddls:
CREATE TABLE "meter_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "dec_address" INTEGER /* Decimal address of the meter data */,
  "hex_address" TEXT /* Hexadecimal representation of the meter data address */,
  "words_count" INTEGER /* Count of words in the data entry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data entry */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the value (e.g., U8, U32) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_meter_data_data_type FOREIGN KEY ("data_type") REFERENCES meter_data_types ("data_type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "tariffs" (
  "tariff_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tariff */,
  "tariff_name" TEXT /* Name of the tariff */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tariff */,
  "rate" REAL /* Rate charged for the tariff */,
  "valid_from" TEXT /* Date when the tariff becomes effective */,
  "valid_to" TEXT /* Date when the tariff expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tariff_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_consumption" (
  "consumption_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each consumption record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding meter data entry */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the energy meter */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was recorded */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the energy consumption */,
  "tariff_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tariff applied to the consumption */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("consumption_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES meter_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_consumption_tariff_id FOREIGN KEY ("tariff_id") REFERENCES tariffs ("tariff_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meters" (
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy meter */,
  "meter_serial" TEXT /* Serial number of the energy meter */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the energy meter installation */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the meter was installed */,
  "meter_type" TEXT /* Type of the energy meter (e.g., smart meter, analog meter) */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the meter owner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("meter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_meters_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES meter_owners ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meter_owners" (
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each meter owner */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the meter owner */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the meter owner */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the meter owner */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the meter owner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "billing" (
  "bill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each billing record */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding meter */,
  "billing_period_start" TEXT /* Start date of the billing period */,
  "billing_period_end" TEXT /* End date of the billing period */,
  "amount_due" REAL /* Total amount due for the billing period */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., paid, pending, overdue) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_billing_meter_id FOREIGN KEY ("meter_id") REFERENCES meters ("meter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment record */,
  "bill_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding bill */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date when the payment was made */,
  "amount_paid" REAL /* Amount paid by the meter owner */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payments_bill_id FOREIGN KEY ("bill_id") REFERENCES billing ("bill_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user account */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the system */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the meter related to the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high usage, faulty meter) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_meter_id FOREIGN KEY ("meter_id") REFERENCES meters ("meter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the meter that was maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance activity */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_meter_id FOREIGN KEY ("meter_id") REFERENCES meters ("meter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Name of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the technician */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meter_data_types" (
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "data_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_type_id")
)
